rationale: The cited paper is used as core prior work establishing that unanticipated cuts in advertising and R&D can reflect myopic real-activity management with negative long-term valuation effects. The citing paper repeatedly positions its contribution relative to this study, explicitly arguing that it adds missing antecedents (analyst forecast pressure) and boundary conditions (agency-theoretic moderators). It also uses Mizik (2010) in measurement/method context for constructing unanticipated budget changes and in interpreting long-run consequences. This is more than a passing background mention and directly informs the paper's framing and empirical setup.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Mizik (2010)'s myopic management line by treating unanticipated advertising and R&D cuts as value-destructive real-activity manipulation. It extends that work by introducing analyst forecasts and agency-cost moderators as drivers of such cuts and by testing additional long-term effects on cash-flow and risk volatility.
